mnemonics: Please Do Not Touch Steve's Pet Alligator (Physical Layer OSI Layer 1: Deals with Physical transmission of raw bits over a medium)
keyPoints: It defines the electrical, mechanical, and functional specifications for the physical link between devices.
It is responsible for the actual transmission and reception of unstructured raw data (bits) over a physical medium.
Key components include the type of cable, connectors, pin layout, voltage levels, and data rates.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Physical Layer's role with the Data Link Layer's; the Physical Layer only deals with bits, not frames or error detection.
Assuming all physical media (like UTP, coaxial, fiber) have the same specifications for speed, distance, and signal type.
